old_claim.note: Insurance Claims Adjustment provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The 1884 system shifted occupational accident and disease claims from individual employer suits to statutory accident insurance institutions.

source_shape.source_locator: Brief History: DGUV says employees previously had to claim compensation directly from employers and, after the 1884 change, could make claims to statutory occupational accident insurance institutions.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: DGUV describes a claims shift from employer fault claims to statutory accident-insurance institutions.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports an insurance-claims edge because the scoped system's core change was institutional processing of occupational accident and disease compensation claims.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: occupational_accident_insurance remains enabled by insurance_claims_adjustment, now with official-source evidence.
would_reject_if: The node is later narrowed to prevention-only workplace safety institutions.
A stronger source shows the 1884 system did not process or assess occupational accident and disease claims.
The insurance_claims_adjustment node is narrowed so tightly to fire insurance that it can no longer serve as a general claims-assessment predecessor.
short_reason: DGUV identifies institutional accident-insurance claims as the core statutory change.
